category_title: Adolescents and adults with moderate to severe ID who use speech and key word signing
minimal_context: The student is participating in a midday mealtime group activity and requesting food items.
dialogue:
TUTOR: It is time for our midday meal. What would you like to drink?

STUDENT: Juice.

TUTOR: Here is your juice. What are you going to eat?

STUDENT: Sandwich. (Picks up the sandwich with both hands)

TUTOR: Do you want some fruit with your sandwich?

STUDENT: Yes, apple. (Communicates exclusively verbally, abandoning spontaneous manual signs entirely while holding the food)

options.A: Spend the midday mealtime working on other tasks and covertly observing the student, intervening minimally, only for disputes or extreme raucousness.
options.B: Implement a highly routinized day with set structures for all activities, including academic, social, play, and midday dining.
options.C: Bolster the generalization of the use of signs across different communication partners and activities during midday mealtimes.
options.D: Make regulative practices explicit so the student knows exactly what to do to demonstrate competency and compliance during mealtimes.
